lesson.menu_title: On the Move -- Getting Around by Public Transport
lesson.menu_desc: Subways, trams and ferries in Rome and Istanbul -- 29 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 21 -- On the Move -- Public Transport
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Marlene Landucci | Lesson 21 -- On the Move

listenings.text: Attention, please. This is Rome Metro, Line B. The next station is Colosseo. For the Colosseum and the Roman Forum, get off here. To change to Line A, stay on the train until Termini station. Doors close on the left. Please have your ticket ready and mind the gap between the train and the platform. The next train to Laurentina leaves in four minutes. Thank you for traveling with Rome Metro.

Welcome to Istanbul. This is a short guide for the T1 tram. To ride the tram, you need an Istanbulkart, a travel card. First, buy the card at the machine near the platform. Then, put money on the card. After that, touch the card to the reader before you get on. The tram goes to Sultanahmet, the Grand Bazaar, and the ferry port. For the islands, take the ferry at the last stop. Have a safe trip, and enjoy the city.

extra_audio.text: First, you walk to the tram stop near your hotel. Then, you buy a round-trip ticket at the ticket machine. After that, you wait on the platform for the tram. Next, you take the tram to the city center. Finally, you get off and walk to the museum.
